qa_pairs.question: What should I do if the upper menu items are greyed out after starting COMSOL in GUI mode?
qa_pairs.answer: If the upper menu items are greyed out and not clickable after starting COMSOL in GUI mode, your `~/.comsol` directory may be corrupted, so you should try deleting it.

qa_pairs.question: How do I start an OnDemand desktop session to use COMSOL?
qa_pairs.answer: You can start an OnDemand desktop session by clicking one of the provided OnDemand links, such as `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca` for NIBI or `https://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca` for TRILLIUM.

qa_pairs.question: What is the OnDemand link for the NIBI cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The OnDemand link for the NIBI cluster is `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the OnDemand link for the TRILLIUM cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The OnDemand link for the TRILLIUM cluster is `https://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I run COMSOL 6.2 or a newer version through an OnDemand desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: To run COMSOL 6.2 or a newer version through an OnDemand desktop session, open a new terminal window in your desktop, then load `StdEnv/2023`, load `comsol/6.4`, and finally, run the `comsol` command.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to run COMSOL 6.1 or an older version via an OnDemand desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: To run COMSOL 6.1 or an older version through an OnDemand desktop session, open a new terminal window, then load `StdEnv/2020`, load `comsol/6.1`, and execute the `comsol` command.
